title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Christine Redfern orchestrates a complex deception to frame Linda Marshall for the murder of Elena Marshall. To manipulate the perceived time of events and create an alibi, Christine tampers with Linda’s watch by setting it twenty minutes fast. She also applies brown sunscreen to her body to disguise her skin tone, later discarding the empty bottle out of a window. To further implicate Kenneth Marshall, the conspirators had previously broken one of his pipes near Elven Bay.

During the execution of the plan, Christine wears a white swimsuit and loose clothing to hide her tanned skin. After resetting Linda's watch to the correct time, she climbs a cliff, hides her clothes, and uses a green hat with a red wig to pose as a corpse on the beach. When Patrick Redfern and Emily Brust arrive by boat, Patrick discovers the "body." Fearing the killer is nearby, Emily rows back to the bay to find the police, leaving Patrick alone. Christine then destroys the hat and wig, hiding the pieces in her swimsuit before returning to the hotel to wash off the sunscreen and change into a tennis outfit. She burns the remains of the hat in Linda's fireplace alongside a calendar page to make it appear as though Linda was performing a witchcraft spell.

While the deception unfolds, Patrick Redfern goes to the "fairy hole" and kills Elena Marshall by clutching her neck. Hercule Poirot explains to Rosamond Darnley that while Patrick had a motive to steal Elena's money, his presence on the beach provided an alibi. Poirot concludes that Christine and Patrick intended to make the child, Linda, a scapegoat by exploiting her belief in magic and her guilt over a ritual she performed.
